- [MCP Client - Elicitation Listener](#ElicitationListener)

### Server

Configuration element to act as an MCP server.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Server_SseServer">Sse Server</a> (Deprecated)</p></li><li><p><a href="#Server_StreamableHttpServer">Streamable Http Server</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name this server uses to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. This isnât the same as the name given to this config element (which is later referenced through config-ref parameters). This name doesnât need to be unique.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version this server uses to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. This doesnât need to match the connector or Mule application version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### SSE Server (Deprecated)

Streamable HTTP transport is recommended.

Exposes the MCP server using the SSE transport.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request timeout.</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Request Timeout unit</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitation Timeout Seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout in seconds for elicitation requests. Elicitation requests sent to the client wait this long for a response before timing out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to the HTTP Listener config used to expose the SSE and messaging endpoints.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sse Endpoint Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The endpoint path clients use to initiate the SSE connection.</p></td><td><p>/sse</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Messages Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to send the messaging endpoint request to.</p></td><td><p>/message</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Sse Response Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Message Response Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitations Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><p>ObjectStore that correlates elicitation requests and responses. For deployments with multiple replicas, configure a distributed object store (using Hazelcast or OSv2) to ensure responses are properly routed to the correct worker. If not specified, a default transient instance is used, which is recommended only for single-instance deployments.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitation Polling Seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Polling interval in seconds used to check for elicitation responses written by another replica. This setting controls how frequently the connector checks for responses that arrived on a different worker.</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Streamable HTTP Server

Exposes the MCP server using the Streamable HTTP transport, as defined in
[Streamable HTTP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request timeout.</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Request Timeout unit</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitation Timeout Seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout in seconds for elicitation requests. Elicitation requests sent to the client wait this long for a response before timing out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to the http:listener-config used to expose the SSE and messaging endpoints.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>Yes</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mcp Endpoint Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The endpoint path clients use to initiate the SSE connection.</p></td><td><p><code>/mcp</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>SSE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Whether responses should be sent by initiating a SSE stream or returning a single application/json body.</p></td><td><p>SSE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Response Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include on all valid responses (not including rejected invalid requests).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for idle sessions.</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit qualifying the sessionIdleTimeout</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sessions Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ObjectStore used to store session information. When deploying to multiple replicas, define a specific object store distributed across all replicas, either through Hazelcast or OSv2. If not specified, a default transient instance is created. Persistence must be enabled if running MCP Server listeners in a multi-worker deployment. For CloudHub 2.0, the Clustered option also must be enabled.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitations Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><p>ObjectStore that correlates elicitation requests and responses. For deployments with multiple replicas, configure a distributed object store (using Hazelcast or OSv2) to ensure responses are properly routed to the correct worker. If not specified, a default transient instance is used, which is recommended only for single-instance deployments.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Elicitation Polling Seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Polling interval in seconds used to check for elicitation responses written by another replica. This setting controls how frequently the connector checks for responses that arrived on a different worker.</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [MCP Server - Create Elicitation](#CreateElicitation)

## Associated Sources

- [MCP Server - On New Session Listener](#OnNewSessionListener)
- [MCP Server - Prompt Listener](#PromptListener)
- [MCP Server - Resource listener](#ResourceListener)
- [MCP Server - Tool Listener](#ToolListener)

## Operations

## MCP Client - Call Tool

`<mcp:call-tool>`

Invokes a tool on a remote MCP server.

Response validation is managed by the underlying SDK; the connector doesnât perform its own
validation.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tool Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the tool to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Arguments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The toolâs input arguments.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Extract Structured Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls how the payload is exposed when a tool returns structured content. When <code>false</code>, the payload is the full <code>CallToolResponse</code> object (previous behavior). When <code>true</code>, if the MCP response has structured content (for example, a tool with an output schema), the payload is the structured object itself so downstream components can reference fields directly (for example, <code>payload.temperature</code>). If structured content is not present and the response has a single text content that is valid JSON, the text is parsed and used as the payload. In all cases, the <code>attributes.rawMcpResponse</code> property contains the entire MCP response in the same format as before. Any validation or schema errors from the SDK are returned to the user as <code>MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED</code> errors.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CallToolResponse">Call Tool Response</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CallToolResultAttributes">Call Tool Result Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Client - Get Prompt

`<mcp:get-prompt>`

Resolves a prompt by name in the remote MCP server and returns the rendered prompt messages. Use
this operation when your Mule application (acting as an MCP client) needs to fetch a prompt that a
remote MCP server exposes, optionally passing arguments that the server uses to render the prompt
content.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the prompt to retrieve from the remote MCP server.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Arguments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Arguments to pass to the prompt. The accepted keys and their types are defined by the remote promptâs argument schema.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional properties to include with the request. Use this field to specify custom key-value pairs that are sent with the MCP connector request. This field is optional and null-safe.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#GetPromptResponse">Get Prompt Response</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Client - List Prompts

`<mcp:list-prompts>`

Lists all prompts available in the remote MCP server. Each entry in the returned array includes
metadata such as the prompt name, optional title, description, and the argument definitions the
prompt accepts.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional properties to include with the request. Use this field to specify custom key-value pairs that are sent with the MCP connector request. This field is optional and null-safe.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#PromptMetadata">Prompt Metadata</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Client - List Resources

`<mcp:list-resources>`

Lists all the available resources in the remote MCP server.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional properties to include with the request. Use this field to specify custom key-value pairs that are sent with the MCP connector request. This field is optional and null-safe.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#ResourceMetadata">Resource Metadata</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:INVALID_URI
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Client - List Tools

`<mcp:list-tools>`

Lists all the available tools in the remote MCP server.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional properties to include with the request. Use this field to specify custom key-value pairs that are sent with the MCP connector request. This field is optional and null-safe.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Client - Ping

`<mcp:ping>`

Pings the remote server.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## MCP Client - Read Resource

`<mcp:read-resource>`

Reads a resource referenced by its URI.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output
