### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- MCP:CONNECTIVITY
- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:INVALID_URI
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## MCP Server - Create Elicitation

`<mcp:create-elicitation>`

Requests an elicitation from the MCP Client. This operation sends an elicitation request via SSE and
blocks until the response is received. In clustered environments, the elicitation response might
arrive on a different worker, in which case it is stored in Object Store and polled.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Session identifier for the connected MCP client.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Prompt message shown to the client.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#BooleanSchemaProperty">Boolean Schema Property</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#EnumSchemaProperty">Enum Schema Property</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#IntegerSchemaProperty">Integer Schema Property</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NumberSchemaProperty">Number Schema Property</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#StringSchemaProperty">String Schema Property</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Input schema presented to the client for elicitation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#ElicitationResult">Elicitation Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Server](#Server)

### Throws

- MCP:INTERNAL_ERROR
- MCP:INVALID_PARAMS
- MCP:INVALID_REQUEST
- MCP:JSON_RPC_ERROR
- MCP:METHOD_NOT_FOUND
- MCP:PARSE_ERROR
- MCP:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- MCP:SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
- MCP:SERVER_ERROR
- MCP:UNAUTHORIZED

## Sources

## MCP Client - Elicitation Listener

`<mcp:elicitation-listener>`

Listens for elicitation requests from MCP servers. When a server requires additional information
during tool or resource execution, this source triggers a flow to collect that data and return it to
the server. The triggered flow receives `ElicitationAttributes` containing the prompt and input
schema, and must return an `ElicitationResponse` object specifying the action to take.

To accept with content:

```none
<ee:transform>
  <ee:message>
    <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[
      %dw 2.0
      output application/java
      ---
      {
        action: "ACCEPT",
        content: {
          firstName: "John",
          lastName: "Doe"
        } as Object {class: "com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.elicitation.ElicitationResponse"}
      }
    ]]></ee:set-payload>
  </ee:message>
</ee:transform>
```

To decline:

```none
<set-variable name="response" value="#[output application/java --- { action: 'DECLINE' as Object {class: 'com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.elicitation.ElicitationResponse'} }]" />
```

To cancel:

```none
<set-variable name="response" value="#[output application/java --- { action: 'CANCEL' as Object {class: 'com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.elicitation.ElicitationResponse'} }]" />
```

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ElicitationResponse">Elicitation Response</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Elicitation response to send back to the server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ElicitationAttributes">Elicitation Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

## MCP Server - On New Session Listener

`<mcp:on-new-session-listener>`

Triggers a flow when a new MCP client session is initiated. For each session connection request, the
flow runs to let you review session details, validate client certificates, or apply custom
authentication logic before choosing to accept or reject the session. When triggered, the flow
receives a message with the session ID, relevant properties (including any custom headers), and
client certificate information if present. To accept the session, let the flow complete
successfully. To reject it, set the rejection parameters with your chosen error message, HTTP status
code, and headers.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reject With Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reject With Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reject With Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#NewSessionRequest">New Session Request</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Server](#Server)

## MCP Server - Prompt Listener

`<mcp:prompt-listener>`

Exposes a prompt whose messages are generated by the execution of the owning flow. The flow is
executed for every received `prompts/get` request. When triggered, the flow runs with a message
whose payload contains the prompt name and the arguments supplied by the client. The messages
returned to the client are produced by the `<mcp:prompt-messages>` nested component.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The prompt name. This value is what clients use to request the prompt.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Title</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A short, human-friendly prompt title.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when this prompt should be used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt Arguments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#PromptArgument">Prompt Argument</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Prompt argument definitions exposed in <code>prompts/list</code>. Each argument can be declared as required or optional and can include a description and a JSON schema that the client uses to validate input.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source only on the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt Messages</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#TextPromptMessageContent">Text Prompt Message Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImagePromptMessageContent">Image Prompt Message Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#EmbeddedResourcePromptMessageContent">Embedded Resource Prompt Message Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ResourceLinkPromptMessageContent">Resource Link Prompt Message Content</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the prompt messages returned to the client. These parameters are evaluated when the flow finishes successfully (when the payload and variables are available).</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#PromptRequest">Prompt Request</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestAttributes">Request Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Server](#Server)

## MCP Server - Resource listener

`<mcp:resource-listener>`

Exposes a resource whose content is generated by the execution of the owning flow. The flow is
executed for every received resources/read request. The user can choose to implement the flow so
that the resource content is static or to make it dynamic. For instance, a dynamic resource could be
a expenses.csv file, that is actually the result of executing a live DB query. Or a static resource
could be a file obtained through Amazon S3 and locally cached using the `ee:cache` component.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the resource.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when to read this resource.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource URI.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource MIME type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Listener Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#TextResource">Text Resource</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#BlobResource">Blob Resource</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the resource content and type. These parameters are evaluated when the flow finishes successfully (when the payload and variables available).</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#ResourceRequest">Resource Request</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestAttributes">Request Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Server](#Server)

## MCP Server - Tool Listener

`<mcp:tool-listener>`

Exposes a tool whose result is generated by the execution of the owning flow. The flow runs for
every received tools/call request. When triggered, the flow runs with a message whose payload is a
Java map holding the input arguments. The tool response is generated when the flow finishes either
successfully or with error. In both cases, the response can include several content items of
different types. If no error response is defined, then a text content item holding the errorâs
description is generated automatically.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The toolâs name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when this tool should be called.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters Schema</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A JSON schema defining an object whose fields act as the toolâs input arguments.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Schema Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#InlineOutputSchema">Inline Output Schema</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#FileOutputSchema">File Output Schema</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Optional output schema definition. Choose between inline JSON schema or a file path reference.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Responses</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#TextToolResponseContent">Text Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageToolResponseContent">Image Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextResourceToolResponseContent">Text Resource Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#BlobResourceToolResponseContent">Blob Resource Tool Response Content</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response items when the flow finishes successfully.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>On Error Responses</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#TextToolResponseContent">Text Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageToolResponseContent">Image Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextResourceToolResponseContent">Text Resource Tool Response Content</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#BlobResourceToolResponseContent">Blob Resource Tool Response Content</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content items when the flow finishes in error.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#RequestAttributes">Request Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Server](#Server)

## Types

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Donât use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Elicitation Attributes

Attributes available in flows triggered by `Elicitation Listener`. Extends
[Request Attributes](#RequestAttributes) to include elicitation-specific fields.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session identifier for the connected MCP client.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique identifier for the elicitation request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional values attached to the request as HTTP headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Certificate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CertificateData">Certificate Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client certificate used to authenticate the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Elicitation Response

Response structure for elicitation requests. This is returned by client flows handling elicitation
requests from servers.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACCEPT</p></li><li><p>DECLINE</p></li><li><p>CANCEL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client response action. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACCEPT</p><div><p>Client provides the requested data in the <strong>Content</strong> field</p></div></li><li><p>DECLINE</p><div><p>Client refuses to provide data and the <strong>Content</strong> field should be null</p></div></li><li><p>CANCEL</p><div><p>Client cancels the entire operation and the <strong>Content</strong> field should be null</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Response content when action is <code>ACCEPT</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Elicitation Result

Result returned by the **Create Elicitation** operation to server flows. Contains the clientâs
response to an elicitation request.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACCEPT</p></li><li><p>DECLINE</p></li><li><p>CANCEL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client response action. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACCEPT</p><div><p>Client provides the requested data in the <strong>Content</strong> field</p></div></li><li><p>DECLINE</p><div><p>Client refuses to provide data and the <strong>Content</strong> field should be null</p></div></li><li><p>CANCEL</p><div><p>Client cancels the entire operation and the operation throws an <code>MCP:OPERATION_CANCELLED</code> error.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Response content when action is <code>ACCEPT</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Boolean Schema Property

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Default Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default value for the property.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property description.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Required</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the property is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Enum Schema Property

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enum Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allowed values for the property.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property description.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Required</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the property is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Integer Schema Property

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum accepted value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum accepted value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property description.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Required</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the property is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Number Schema Property

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum accepted value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum accepted value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property description.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Required</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the property is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### String Schema Property

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Min Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum string length.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum string length.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Validation regex pattern.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property description.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Required</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the property is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Call Tool Response

Response obtained from calling a tool as a client.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Contents</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#CallToolResponseContent">Call Tool Response Content</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The contents generated by the tool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the tool execution was successful or errored out. Notice that even in case of error, contents might have been generated.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Call Tool Response Content

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>text</p></li><li><p>image</p></li><li><p>resource</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contentâs payload when type is <code>text</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contentâs MIME type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contentâs payload when type is <code>image</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ResourceContent">Resource Content</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contentâs payload when type is <code>resource</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audience</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>ASSISTANT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes who the intended consumer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of <code>1</code> means "most important", which indicates that the data is required. A value of <code>0</code> means "least important", which indicates that the data is entirely optional.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Resource Content

A flattened representation of a resource content. The resource can be either text or blob types. The
text property is set for text resources, while the blob type is used for the latter.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource MIME type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of a text resource. Null for BLOB data types.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blob</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of a BLOB resource. Null for text data types.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Resource Metadata

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the resource.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description of the resource.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the resource.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory to allocate to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the Buffer Size Increment value, with an upper limit of the Max In Memory Size value. instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>100</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>100</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of instances to keep in memory. If more than the maximum is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Objects</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tool Metadata

Describes a tool through its properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the tool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when to call this tool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Schema</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JSON Schema describing the toolâs input parameters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### New Session Request

Contains the data associated to a client requiring the creation of a new server session.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Certificate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CertificateData">Certificate Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Certificate Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Basic Constraints</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Critical Extension OI Ds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoded</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Extended Key Usage</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#CertificateExtension">Certificate Extension</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Issuer Alternative Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#AlternativeNameData">Alternative Name Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Issuer DN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PrincipalData">Principal Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Issuer Unique ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Issuer X500 Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#X500PrincipalData">X500 Principal Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Usage</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Critical Extension OI Ds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not After</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Date</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Date</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PublicKeyData">Public Key Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serial Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serial Number Object</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SerialNumberData">Serial Number Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sig Alg Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sig Alg OID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sig Alg Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Signature</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject Alternative Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#AlternativeNameData">Alternative Name Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject DN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PrincipalData">Principal Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject X500 Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#X500PrincipalData">X500 Principal Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Certificate Extension

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Criticality</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Oid</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Alternative Name Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Principal Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Common Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### X500 Principal Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Public Key Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoded</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modulus</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Serial Number Data

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Serial Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine if a message was redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures how to identify a redelivered message and how to find out when the message was
redelivered.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. You can set this property only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Resource Request

An inbound request for a resource, used when the connector is acting as an MCP server.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Requested Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Request Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional values attached to the request as HTTP Headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Certificate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CertificateData">Certificate Data</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client certificate used to authenticate the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Text Tool Response Content

Defines a text content thatâs part of the response of a tool implemented through this connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text content.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audience</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>ASSISTANT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes who the intended consumer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content thatâs useful for multiple audiences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of <code>1</code> means "most important", which indicates that the data is required. A value of <code>0</code> means "least important", which indicates that the data is entirely optional.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Image Tool Response Content

Defines an image content thatâs part of the response of a tool implemented through this connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Audience</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>ASSISTANT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes who the intended consumer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of <code>1</code> means "most important", which indicates that the data is required. A value of <code>0</code> means "least important", which indicates that the data is entirely optional.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The raw image content, without Base64 encoding.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The image MIME type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Text Resource Tool Response Content

Defines an embedded text resource thatâs part of the response of a tool implemented through this
connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Audience</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>ASSISTANT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes who the intended consumer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of <code>1</code> means "most important", which indicates that the data is required. A value of <code>0</code> means "least important", which indicates that the data is entirely optional.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The resource MIME type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource text.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Blob Resource Tool Response Content

Defines an embedded blob resource thatâs part of the response of a tool implemented through this
connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Audience</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>ASSISTANT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes who the intended consumer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of <code>1</code> means "most important", which indicates that the data is required. A value of <code>0</code> means "least important", which indicates that the data is entirely optional.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blob</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The raw resource content, without Base64 encoding.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The resource MIME type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Text Resource

Defines a text resource.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text content.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
